pages.text: $(flesh)Membranes$() are biomancy's selective walls — blocks that look solid (or glassy) but let $(italic)some$() things through and stop others. They are how you build a base that sorts its own traffic.
pages.title: Membranes & Gates

pages.text: $(bold)Permeable membranes$() pass creatures by life-stage or kind:$(br)$(li)$(flesh)Baby-Permeable$() — only babies pass.$(li)$(flesh)Adult-Permeable$() — only adults pass.$(li)$(flesh)Undead-Permeable$() — only the undead pass.$(li)$(flesh)Primal Membrane$() — keyed to biomancy's own primordial creatures.$(li)$(flesh)Impermeable Membrane$() — a flesh wall nothing passes.

$(bold)The Biometric Membrane$() is the precise one. Keyed with a creature's $(l:5_arms/essence)Essence$(), it passes only that species — or, with a Unique Essence, only that one individual. An $(flesh)inverted$() membrane does the opposite: it stops the keyed creature and passes all others.$(br2)Each membrane also comes as a thinner $(flesh)pane$(), and panes and full blocks craft back and forth freely.
pages.title: The Biometric Membrane

pages.text: $(bold)The Modular Larynx$() is a stranger gate — a block that speaks. Placed on soul sand or soil it cries a death sound; on magma, a hurt sound; on anything else, an ambient one. A mob-scaring noisemaker, set by what you stand it on.
pages.title: The Modular Larynx
